title: settings drawer enters from LEFT — explicit direction for thin client wiring
body:
Clarification on direction for the settings drawer.

The settings drawer enters from the LEFT side of the iframe — the OPPOSITE of the wallet drawer which enters from the right.

CSS already in MetaMeRuntimeClient (do not change this):
  absolute left-0 top-0 bottom-0 z-50 w-80
  closed: -translate-x-full  (off-screen to the LEFT)
  open:    translate-x-0     (slides IN from the left edge)

This means:
- Closed state: drawer is at translateX(-100%) — hidden off the left edge
- Open state:   drawer is at translateX(0)    — flush with the left edge of the iframe
- Transition: slides RIGHT into view from the left edge (enters from left)

The wallet drawer does the opposite: right-0, translate-x-full closed, slides in from the right.

For the thin client settings button (apps/metame-runtime-shell/) — no change needed to the direction. Just call:
  postShellEvent('MENU_ACTION', { action_id: 'settings' })
The iframe handles the left-entering animation.
